untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a conversion arbitrage pricing formula (Stock + Put − Strike − Call + Carrying cost − Dividends) rather than a technical entry/exit condition. The rule requires: (1) real-time option prices (Put, Call) and strike prices, which are not available in the allowed indicator set; (2) carrying cost rate, time held, and dividend amounts as external parameters, not market-derived indicators; (3) calculation of absolute arbitrage profit rather than directional price movement. The backtest engine lacks option chain data, strike price definitions, and carrying cost inputs needed to implement this formula-based reversal strategy.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_188 is a conversion arbitrage formula from options trading, not a technical pattern signal. It requires derivatives pricing inputs (option Greeks, strike definitions) and portfolio-level cost accounting (carrying costs, dividends) that fall outside the scope of a price-action backtester. This would require a dedicated derivatives pricing engine with options data feeds.
